gcmotion.Plots#

class gcmotion.Plots(collection)#

Component of class Collection. Contains all the plotting-related methods.

__init__(collection)#

Copies attributes from collection to self.

The instance itself is automatically initialized internally by the Collection class, and only its methods are to be called by the user, as collection.plot.<method()>.

Parameters:

collection (Particle) – The Particle collection

Methods

__init__(collection)

Copies attributes from collection to self.

contour_energy([theta_lim, psi_lim, ...])

Creates contour plot with the particles' drifts on top.

parabolas([different_colors, labels])

Plots the orbit type parabolas and the particles' orbit type points.

poincare([angle, lim])

_check_multiples(allowed: list) → bool#

Checks if the given parameters are static or vary from particle to particle.

Since all the plots require some parameters to be the same and allowing only certain parameters to vary from particle to particle, this check is important. Otherwise the resulting plots are nonsense.

Parameters:

allowed (list) – The parameters which are alloed to vary.

Returns:

Truth value depending on the result of the check.

Return type:

bool

contour_energy(theta_lim: list = [-3.141592653589793, 3.141592653589793], psi_lim: str | list = 'auto', plot_drift: bool = True, contour_Phi: bool = True, units: Literal['normal', 'eV', 'keV'] = 'keV', levels: int = None, wall_shade: bool = True, **kwargs)#

Creates contour plot with the particles’ drifts on top.

Uses mostly the already existing plotting methods from Particle

The particles must have the same R, a, qfactor, efield, bfield, mu, Pz0 and be the same species.

Parameters:

  • theta_lim (list, optional) – Plot xlim. Must be either [0,2π] or [-π,π]. Defaults to [-np.pi, np.pi].

  • psi_lim (str | list, optional) – If a list is passed, it plots between the 2 values relative to \(\psi_{wall}\). Defaults to “auto”.

  • plot_drift (bool, optional) – Whether or not to plot \(\theta - P_\theta\) drift on top.. Defaults to True.

  • contour_Phi (bool, optional) – Whether or not to add the Φ term in the energy contour.. Defaults to True.

  • units (str, optional) – The energy units. Must be ‘normal’, ‘eV’ or ‘keV’. Defaults to keV. Defaults to “keV”.

  • levels (int, optional) – The number of contour levels.. Defaults to None.

  • wall_shade (bool, optional) – Whether to shade the region \(\psi/ \psi_{wall} > 1\). Defaults to True.

parabolas(different_colors=True, labels=False)#

Plots the orbit type parabolas and the particles’ orbit type points.

Parameters:

  • different_colors (bool, optional) – Whether or not to use different different color for each drift and colorbar label. Defaults to True.

  • labels (bool, optional) – Whether or not to print the particles’ labels above their orbit type points. Defaults to False.
